texto: I have seen many die, surrounded by loved ones, and their last words were ‘I love you.’ There were some who could no longer speak yet with their eyes and soft smile left behind that same healing message. I have been in rooms where those who were dying made it feel like sacred ground. (26)

texto: You have to remember one life, one death–this one! To enter fully the day, the hour, the moment whether it appears as life or death, whether we catch it on the inbreath or outbreath, requires only a moment, this moment. And along with it all the mindfulness we can muster, and each stage of our ongoing birth, and the confident joy of our inherent luminosity. (24)
explicacao: Orientação direta de Stephen Levine sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “You have to remember one life, one death–this one! To enter fully the day, the hour, the moment w…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, confidence, death, útil em momentos de luto e death.

texto: Often when we hear people speak about meditation, we hear about wisdom, we hear about knowledge. But what, actually, is the effect, what’s the use, of wisdom or knowledge? Understanding. When you understand mind, you’re not at its mercy. When you don’t understand, you’re lost in the midst of it.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Stephen Levine que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Often when we hear people speak about meditation, we hear about wisdom, we hear about knowledge. …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, meditation, about, com foco em reflexao.

texto: Until we find out who was born this time around, it seems irrelevant to seek earlier identities. I have heard many people speak of who they believe they were in previous incarnations, but they seem to have very little idea of who they are in this one. . . . Let’s take one life at a time. Perhaps the best way to do that is to live as though there were no afterlife or reincarnation. To live as though this moment was all that was allotted. (132)
explicacao: Citação de Stephen Levine sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Until we find out who was born this time around, it seems irrelevant to seek earlier identities. …” articula vida, afterlife, born em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como born e vida.

texto: Our life is composed of events and states of mind. How ewe appraise our life from our deathbed will be predicated not only on what came to us in life but how we lived with it. (34)
